programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-2 Chinese Wrestling
programme.desc: In Episode 2, Chinese Wrestling, host Kleber joins a public training session led by Wang Tongqing, a master preserving the imperial Shanpu Ying wrestling tradition. Swept up in the group’s lively atmosphere, he gets a first-hand feel for the master’s skill. Kleber then invites his Italian friend Paolo to train systematically, uncovering how Chinese wrestling principles connect with other martial arts, from Tai Chi to Wing Chun. Through Master Wang’s story of bringing a formerly imperial martial art into the public sphere, mentoring the next generation, and hoping to take it onto the international stage, the episode shows wrestling as more than a test of strength. It is a cultural bridge between history and the present.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Pak Mei
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber seeks out Liu Weixin, a master of Pak Mei kungfu and on whom the hit game "Sifu" was based. He experiences for himself this style's relentless and lightning-fast strikes, but also learns from its master how to balance life and kungfu, how a master should treat his disciples, and what it means to "eat porridge at night"

programme.title: Ningxia: Echoes of the Yellow River
programme.desc: Ningxia: Echoes of the Yellow River is a documentary focusing on the World Heritage sites and cultural–ecological conservation in the Ningxia Hui Autonomous Region. Through the lens of Ningxia’s heritage stories, the film highlights China’s commitment to World Heritage preservation, ecological restoration, and cultural continuity — presenting to global audiences a land where history and modernity intertwine, and where desert and oasis coexist. By following the guardians of the Western Xia Imperial Tombs, desert restorers in the Tengger Desert, Great Wall rangers at Sanguankou, traditional sheepskin raft craftsmen, and managers of the Yellow River irrigation system in Qingtongxia, the program reveals Ningxia’s distinctive contributions to cultural heritage, natural ecology, and intangible traditions — and the enduring human spirit behind them.

programme.title: China Untapped: Nanjing 3
programme.desc: Join us in Nanjing’s bustling old quarter, where you can try on silk dresses worth their weight in gold, and see how traditional handicrafts have been given modern twists. We’ll feast on street snacks and local delicacies by the old Confucian Temple, before taking a pleasure cruise along Nanjing’s mother river to round off our exploration of this vibrant and historic city.

programme.title: Life as a Miao Silversmith
programme.desc: In this episode, Eva May steps into Maliao Village — a century-old silversmith village nestled in Leishan, Guizhou Province. Over the course of a week, she dives into the craft and daily lives of Miao men who carry on the silversmithing legacy. She works side by side with local silversmiths day in and day out, delving into the culture and techniques behind Miao silverware. She even joins master silversmiths for a live-stream event, before engaging in discussions with university professors about how traditional craftsmanship can fuel rural revitalization. On top of that, she witnesses the breathtaking sight of "maidens adorned in silver and flowers" at the grand Miao New Year celebrations, visits Xijiang Qianhu Miaozhai — the world’s largest Miao village — and learns to forge a small yet meaningful piece of Miao silver ornament.

programme.title: Young Shaolin master
programme.desc: Xu Haican is a kung fu practitioner at the Quanzhou Shaolin Temple in Fujian. The 12-year-old boy is now an expert with the sword, the nine-section chain whip, and the monkey stick after just two years of training. Xu said these weapons give him both confidence and discipline, as he must wield them safely in practice. The young Shaolin master said he hopes to learn more from his masters and carry forward the Chinese martial art.

programme.title: Foodwise: Chongqing
programme.desc: Welcome to Chongqing, China's largest city. Located in the country's southwest, it straddles two mighty rivers and is famed for its extraordinary skyline, with high-rises mushrooming out of the hills and vertigo-inducing bridges.But Tianran He and Angela Fu are here for the food: From xiaomian noodles and Jianghu cuisine to the city's most famous dish: Chongqing hotpot, our hosts take on the spicy, numbing and scintillating aromas of Chongqing cuisine. We promise you: Chongqing will electrify your senses and tantalise your taste buds.

programme.title: Foodwise: Veggie Beijing
programme.desc: Beijing is a 3000-year old metropolis home to a dizzying array of culinary traditions, not least of which is vegetarian cuisine. For Beijingers, eating vegetarian is the best way to welcome spring. In this episode, Tianran and Angela take up the challenge: From simple home cooking utilizing spring flowers and leaves, to the extravagant mock-meat masterpieces of imperial cuisine, and international dishes well-known to the vegetarian community, our hosts discover a plethora of plant-based options to be found amongst the historic alleys and skyscrapers of China's capital.

programme.title: China Highly Recommended: Changsha
programme.desc: Episode 1 of Travelogue's "China Highly Recommended" tells the story of Changsha, one of China's happiest cities. Following the lives of four of its foreign residents: A French executive chef, a Japanese podcaster, and two American teachers, we discover the reasons why they made Changsha their second home, and explore the city with them as only locals do. This episode will be released as a 30 minute travel documentary to be broadcast on CGTN's television channel and social media platform, along with a series of video clips and photos

programme.title: A Sip of Mount Wuyi
programme.desc: Wuyi rock tea, nurtured in the mineral-rich cliffs of the Wuyi Mountains, is a distinctive oolong embodying a unique "rock rhyme" (yan yun)—a taste of nature's harmony. More than a beverage, teas like Da Hong Pao invite mindful appreciation, reflecting centuries of tradition and a philosophy of patience in their complex roasted notes and enduring aftertaste. Discover the terroir, meticulous processing, and the spirit of Chinese tea culture within these exceptional leaves.

programme.title: Qingdao: The Jade Island
programme.desc: To boost Qingdao’s international‑oriented publicity and build its profile as an international tourist city, this episode takes us to Qingdao. Through three segments‑‑Modern Qingdao, Historic Qingdao and Internationalized Qingdao, we showcase the diverse facets of this charming city.

programme.title: Young Shaolin master
programme.desc: Xu Haican is a kung fu practitioner at the Quanzhou Shaolin Temple in Fujian. The 12-year-old boy is now an expert with the sword, the nine-section chain whip, and the monkey stick after just two years of training. Xu said these weapons give him both confidence and discipline, as he must wield them safely in practice. The young Shaolin master said he hopes to learn more from his masters and carry forward the Chinese martial art.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-3 Shaolin Style
programme.desc: Episode 3, Shaolin, explores how this thousand-year-old temple has evolved into a unique “kung fu platform” that draws enthusiasts from around the world. Host Kleber immerses himself in Shaolin’s integrated way of life, where Chan (Zen) practice and martial training go hand in hand, and turns the camera toward foreign practitioners pursuing their kung fu dreams both inside and beyond the temple gates. Along the way, he meets international disciples living and training at Shaolin, as well as students learning the rare art of Shaolin Xinyiba under Master Hu Zhengsheng, a former Shaolin monk. At martial arts schools near Songshan Mountain, Kleber also encounters overseas competitors training hard for the arena. Together, their stories reveal Shaolin kung fu’s enduring pull, one that crosses cultures and borders while uniting practitioners through tradition, discipline, and self-mastery.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Pak Mei

programme.title: China Highly Recommended: Changsha
programme.desc: Episode 1 of Travelogue's "China Highly Recommended" tells the story of Changsha, one of China's happiest cities. Following the lives of four of its foreign residents: A French executive chef, a Japanese podcaster, and two American teachers, we discover the reasons why they made Changsha their second home, and explore the city with them as only locals do. This episode will be released as a 30 minute travel documentary to be broadcast on CGTN's television channel and social media platform, along with a series of video clips and photos

programme.title: A Sip of Mount Wuyi
programme.desc: Wuyi rock tea, nurtured in the mineral-rich cliffs of the Wuyi Mountains, is a distinctive oolong embodying a unique "rock rhyme" (yan yun)—a taste of nature's harmony. More than a beverage, teas like Da Hong Pao invite mindful appreciation, reflecting centuries of tradition and a philosophy of patience in their complex roasted notes and enduring aftertaste. Discover the terroir, meticulous processing, and the spirit of Chinese tea culture within these exceptional leaves.

programme.title: Qingdao: The Jade Island
programme.desc: To boost Qingdao’s international‑oriented publicity and build its profile as an international tourist city, this episode takes us to Qingdao. Through three segments‑‑Modern Qingdao, Historic Qingdao and Internationalized Qingdao, we showcase the diverse facets of this charming city.

programme.title: Young Shaolin master
programme.desc: Xu Haican is a kung fu practitioner at the Quanzhou Shaolin Temple in Fujian. The 12-year-old boy is now an expert with the sword, the nine-section chain whip, and the monkey stick after just two years of training. Xu said these weapons give him both confidence and discipline, as he must wield them safely in practice. The young Shaolin master said he hopes to learn more from his masters and carry forward the Chinese martial art.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-3 Shaolin Style
programme.desc: Episode 3, Shaolin, explores how this thousand-year-old temple has evolved into a unique “kung fu platform” that draws enthusiasts from around the world. Host Kleber immerses himself in Shaolin’s integrated way of life, where Chan (Zen) practice and martial training go hand in hand, and turns the camera toward foreign practitioners pursuing their kung fu dreams both inside and beyond the temple gates. Along the way, he meets international disciples living and training at Shaolin, as well as students learning the rare art of Shaolin Xinyiba under Master Hu Zhengsheng, a former Shaolin monk. At martial arts schools near Songshan Mountain, Kleber also encounters overseas competitors training hard for the arena. Together, their stories reveal Shaolin kung fu’s enduring pull, one that crosses cultures and borders while uniting practitioners through tradition, discipline, and self-mastery.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Pak Mei
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber seeks out Liu Weixin, a master of Pak Mei kungfu and on whom the hit game "Sifu" was based. He experiences for himself this style's relentless and lightning-fast strikes, but also learns from its master how to balance life and kungfu, how a master should treat his disciples, and what it means to "eat porridge at night"

programme.title: Ningxia: Echoes of the Yellow River
programme.desc: Ningxia: Echoes of the Yellow River is a documentary focusing on the World Heritage sites and cultural–ecological conservation in the Ningxia Hui Autonomous Region. Through the lens of Ningxia’s heritage stories, the film highlights China’s commitment to World Heritage preservation, ecological restoration, and cultural continuity — presenting to global audiences a land where history and modernity intertwine, and where desert and oasis coexist. By following the guardians of the Western Xia Imperial Tombs, desert restorers in the Tengger Desert, Great Wall rangers at Sanguankou, traditional sheepskin raft craftsmen, and managers of the Yellow River irrigation system in Qingtongxia, the program reveals Ningxia’s distinctive contributions to cultural heritage, natural ecology, and intangible traditions — and the enduring human spirit behind them.

programme.title: China Untapped: Nanjing 3
programme.desc: Join us in Nanjing’s bustling old quarter, where you can try on silk dresses worth their weight in gold, and see how traditional handicrafts have been given modern twists. We’ll feast on street snacks and local delicacies by the old Confucian Temple, before taking a pleasure cruise along Nanjing’s mother river to round off our exploration of this vibrant and historic city.

programme.title: Life as a Miao Silversmith
programme.desc: In this episode, Eva May steps into Maliao Village — a century-old silversmith village nestled in Leishan, Guizhou Province. Over the course of a week, she dives into the craft and daily lives of Miao men who carry on the silversmithing legacy. She works side by side with local silversmiths day in and day out, delving into the culture and techniques behind Miao silverware. She even joins master silversmiths for a live-stream event, before engaging in discussions with university professors about how traditional craftsmanship can fuel rural revitalization. On top of that, she witnesses the breathtaking sight of "maidens adorned in silver and flowers" at the grand Miao New Year celebrations, visits Xijiang Qianhu Miaozhai — the world’s largest Miao village — and learns to forge a small yet meaningful piece of Miao silver ornament.

programme.title: Young Shaolin master
programme.desc: Xu Haican is a kung fu practitioner at the Quanzhou Shaolin Temple in Fujian. The 12-year-old boy is now an expert with the sword, the nine-section chain whip, and the monkey stick after just two years of training. Xu said these weapons give him both confidence and discipline, as he must wield them safely in practice. The young Shaolin master said he hopes to learn more from his masters and carry forward the Chinese martial art.

programme.title: Foodwise: Chongqing
programme.desc: Welcome to Chongqing, China's largest city. Located in the country's southwest, it straddles two mighty rivers and is famed for its extraordinary skyline, with high-rises mushrooming out of the hills and vertigo-inducing bridges.But Tianran He and Angela Fu are here for the food: From xiaomian noodles and Jianghu cuisine to the city's most famous dish: Chongqing hotpot, our hosts take on the spicy, numbing and scintillating aromas of Chongqing cuisine. We promise you: Chongqing will electrify your senses and tantalise your taste buds.

programme.title: Foodwise: Veggie Beijing
programme.desc: Beijing is a 3000-year old metropolis home to a dizzying array of culinary traditions, not least of which is vegetarian cuisine. For Beijingers, eating vegetarian is the best way to welcome spring. In this episode, Tianran and Angela take up the challenge: From simple home cooking utilizing spring flowers and leaves, to the extravagant mock-meat masterpieces of imperial cuisine, and international dishes well-known to the vegetarian community, our hosts discover a plethora of plant-based options to be found amongst the historic alleys and skyscrapers of China's capital.

programme.title: Qingdao: The Jade Island
programme.desc: To boost Qingdao’s international‑oriented publicity and build its profile as an international tourist city, this episode takes us to Qingdao. Through three segments‑‑Modern Qingdao, Historic Qingdao and Internationalized Qingdao, we showcase the diverse facets of this charming city.

programme.title: Young Shaolin master
programme.desc: Xu Haican is a kung fu practitioner at the Quanzhou Shaolin Temple in Fujian. The 12-year-old boy is now an expert with the sword, the nine-section chain whip, and the monkey stick after just two years of training. Xu said these weapons give him both confidence and discipline, as he must wield them safely in practice. The young Shaolin master said he hopes to learn more from his masters and carry forward the Chinese martial art.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-3 Shaolin Style
programme.desc: Episode 3, Shaolin, explores how this thousand-year-old temple has evolved into a unique “kung fu platform” that draws enthusiasts from around the world. Host Kleber immerses himself in Shaolin’s integrated way of life, where Chan (Zen) practice and martial training go hand in hand, and turns the camera toward foreign practitioners pursuing their kung fu dreams both inside and beyond the temple gates. Along the way, he meets international disciples living and training at Shaolin, as well as students learning the rare art of Shaolin Xinyiba under Master Hu Zhengsheng, a former Shaolin monk. At martial arts schools near Songshan Mountain, Kleber also encounters overseas competitors training hard for the arena. Together, their stories reveal Shaolin kung fu’s enduring pull, one that crosses cultures and borders while uniting practitioners through tradition, discipline, and self-mastery.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Hung Ga and Choy Li Fut
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber meets with two masters of southern Chinese martial arts. He learns from a Hung Ga master that while this kungfu style is often associated with brute force, it’s actually a combination of hard and soft techniques that can also be used in traditional Chinese massage. Next, Kleber meets a master of Choy Li Fut, a kungfu style that utilizes everyday items such as towels, fans and even abacuses, and discovers the link between martial arts and lion dance.

programme.title: Jingmai Wanderings: A Symphony of Humans and Tea
programme.desc: As a UNESCO World Heritage site, Mount Jingmai embodies the ecological wisdom of forest–tea symbiosis and the rich cultural diversity of its ethnic communities. It plays a vital role in biodiversity conservation and the preservation of traditional tea culture. This episode follows the stories of representative individuals to explore three key themes — heritage preservation, ecological sustainability, and youth empowerment — revealing the unique charm and sustainable development model of Mount Jingmai.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.1
programme.desc: Join Tianran He in Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. Fed by the Yarlung Zangbo River and abundant rainfall, this lush subtropical region is home to some of Xizan’g most unique scenery. From sacred mountains and ancient monasteries, to cruising along Nyingchi’s mother river, wandering alpine meadows and savoring stone pot chicken – a beloved local delicacy, Tianran uncovers the beauty of Nyingchi.

programme.title: Valentine’s for the Pumi People
programme.desc: In this episode, we record the grand celebration of the traditional Pumi Valentine's Day in Luoguqing, Lanping County, Yunnan Province. Every year, on the fifth day of the fifth lunar month, young men and women from the local community gather on the vast meadows of Luoguqing to take part in singing and dancing activities in pursuit of marriage. During the program, the host interacts with local people of the Pumi and other ethnic groups, showcasing the traditional plateau lifestyle of the Pumi people, their distinctive firepit culture, as well as the community’s brand-new look in the new era. All these scenes unfold against the lively backdrop of the Valentine’s Day activities, fostering a warm atmosphere of ethnic solidarity.

programme.title: Ancient tradition in an old town takes on new life
programme.desc: The Chinese lion dance is an art form that combines acrobatics, dance, folk music and embroidery. The southern style lion dance was a staple in the ancient town of Qiantong before the costume faded in the early 20th century. However, now the tradition has been revived by some incredible talented young people from this town.

programme.title: Foodwise: Great Walls of Beijing
programme.desc: The Great Wall of China is a popular tourist destination, but did you know that in Beijing, it's where locals go to unwind? From feasts of tofu made using well-water and fish caught fresh from the reservoir, to bubbling cauldrons of thinly-sliced lamb, the hilly suburbs of Beijing are home to some of the city's best-loved weekend escapes - and they're all by the foot of the Great Wall.

programme.title: Foodwise: How to Order Chinese Food
programme.desc: How do you order Chinese food? What's the best way to pair dishes? And what's up with the naming system? In this episode, hosts Tianran and Eva will give you a crash course on everything from deciphering the menu and choosing the right restaurant, to pairing dishes according to color, aroma, taste, and form, so that by the end of the show, you too can feast your friends like a pro.

programme.title: Thrill Seeker’s Guide to China: Mount Siguniang
programme.desc: In this episode, Host Tianran He is on a mission: To reach the summit of Mount Siguniang, a towering snowcapped mountain in southwestern China. Together with his friend Ma Chunlin, a photographer for National Geographic, they trek through one of China’s most beautiful and biodiverse hiking routes, before making for base camp. But with worsening weather conditions and severe altitude sickness, the odds are stacked against them. Ten years ago, Tianran failed his attempt at climbing Mount Siguniang’s First Peak – this time, he’s all in.

programme.title: Pingyao Hand-Polished Lacquer Art
programme.desc: Pingyao is an old town with a history of over 2,700 years in the north of China, in Shanxi Province. The rich cultural heritage it built up over the centuries eventually gave rise to a very special art—Tuiguang lacquerware. This hand-polished lacquerware, unique to Pingyao, is recognized as one of the four representative lacquerwares of China.

programme.title: Beijing Trough Art
programme.desc: When it comes to Beijing’s art, you may picture the Forbidden City and ancient porcelain pieces. In fact, Beijing hosts numerous outstanding modern‑art museums, whose buildings are works of art in their own right. Especially in pivotal period,

programme.title: Ancient tradition in an old town takes on new life
programme.desc: The Chinese lion dance is an art form that combines acrobatics, dance, folk music and embroidery. The southern style lion dance was a staple in the ancient town of Qiantong before the costume faded in the early 20th century. However, now the tradition has been revived by some incredible talented young people from this town.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-4 Medieval Combat
programme.desc: Episode 4 centres on Shanghai, where Eastern and Western martial traditions meet in surprising ways. Kleber visits local practitioners of Historical European Martial Arts (HEMA), trying German and Italian longsword techniques first-hand and seeing how Chinese enthusiasts innovate by combining European swordsmanship with ancient Chinese military manuals. The episode also follows the rise of BUHURT, a European-style armored combat sport now gaining traction in China, featuring locally made traditional Chinese armour in intense full-contact bouts. Through Kleber’s own multicultural martial arts studio and the city’s everyday East-West blend, the episode shows Shanghai as a global hub, a place where classical martial arts find new life and cross-cultural practices thrive.

programme.title: Thrill Seeker’s Guide to China: Mount Siguniang
programme.desc: In this episode, Host Tianran He is on a mission: To reach the summit of Mount Siguniang, a towering snowcapped mountain in southwestern China. Together with his friend Ma Chunlin, a photographer for National Geographic, they trek through one of China’s most beautiful and biodiverse hiking routes, before making for base camp. But with worsening weather conditions and severe altitude sickness, the odds are stacked against them. Ten years ago, Tianran failed his attempt at climbing Mount Siguniang’s First Peak – this time, he’s all in.

programme.title: Pingyao Hand-Polished Lacquer Art
programme.desc: Pingyao is an old town with a history of over 2,700 years in the north of China, in Shanxi Province. The rich cultural heritage it built up over the centuries eventually gave rise to a very special art—Tuiguang lacquerware. This hand-polished lacquerware, unique to Pingyao, is recognized as one of the four representative lacquerwares of China.

programme.title: Beijing Trough Art
programme.desc: When it comes to Beijing’s art, you may picture the Forbidden City and ancient porcelain pieces. In fact, Beijing hosts numerous outstanding modern‑art museums, whose buildings are works of art in their own right. Especially in pivotal period,

programme.title: Ancient tradition in an old town takes on new life
programme.desc: The Chinese lion dance is an art form that combines acrobatics, dance, folk music and embroidery. The southern style lion dance was a staple in the ancient town of Qiantong before the costume faded in the early 20th century. However, now the tradition has been revived by some incredible talented young people from this town.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-4 Medieval Combat
programme.desc: Episode 4 centres on Shanghai, where Eastern and Western martial traditions meet in surprising ways. Kleber visits local practitioners of Historical European Martial Arts (HEMA), trying German and Italian longsword techniques first-hand and seeing how Chinese enthusiasts innovate by combining European swordsmanship with ancient Chinese military manuals. The episode also follows the rise of BUHURT, a European-style armored combat sport now gaining traction in China, featuring locally made traditional Chinese armour in intense full-contact bouts. Through Kleber’s own multicultural martial arts studio and the city’s everyday East-West blend, the episode shows Shanghai as a global hub, a place where classical martial arts find new life and cross-cultural practices thrive.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Hung Ga and Choy Li Fut
programme.desc: In Foshan, China, Kleber meets with two masters of southern Chinese martial arts. He learns from a Hung Ga master that while this kungfu style is often associated with brute force, it’s actually a combination of hard and soft techniques that can also be used in traditional Chinese massage. Next, Kleber meets a master of Choy Li Fut, a kungfu style that utilizes everyday items such as towels, fans and even abacuses, and discovers the link between martial arts and lion dance.

programme.title: Jingmai Wanderings: A Symphony of Humans and Tea
programme.desc: As a UNESCO World Heritage site, Mount Jingmai embodies the ecological wisdom of forest–tea symbiosis and the rich cultural diversity of its ethnic communities. It plays a vital role in biodiversity conservation and the preservation of traditional tea culture. This episode follows the stories of representative individuals to explore three key themes — heritage preservation, ecological sustainability, and youth empowerment — revealing the unique charm and sustainable development model of Mount Jingmai.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.1
programme.desc: Join Tianran He in Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. Fed by the Yarlung Zangbo River and abundant rainfall, this lush subtropical region is home to some of Xizan’g most unique scenery. From sacred mountains and ancient monasteries, to cruising along Nyingchi’s mother river, wandering alpine meadows and savoring stone pot chicken – a beloved local delicacy, Tianran uncovers the beauty of Nyingchi.

programme.title: Valentine’s for the Pumi People
programme.desc: In this episode, we record the grand celebration of the traditional Pumi Valentine's Day in Luoguqing, Lanping County, Yunnan Province. Every year, on the fifth day of the fifth lunar month, young men and women from the local community gather on the vast meadows of Luoguqing to take part in singing and dancing activities in pursuit of marriage. During the program, the host interacts with local people of the Pumi and other ethnic groups, showcasing the traditional plateau lifestyle of the Pumi people, their distinctive firepit culture, as well as the community’s brand-new look in the new era. All these scenes unfold against the lively backdrop of the Valentine’s Day activities, fostering a warm atmosphere of ethnic solidarity.

programme.title: Ancient tradition in an old town takes on new life
programme.desc: The Chinese lion dance is an art form that combines acrobatics, dance, folk music and embroidery. The southern style lion dance was a staple in the ancient town of Qiantong before the costume faded in the early 20th century. However, now the tradition has been revived by some incredible talented young people from this town.

programme.title: Foodwise: Great Walls of Beijing
programme.desc: The Great Wall of China is a popular tourist destination, but did you know that in Beijing, it's where locals go to unwind? From feasts of tofu made using well-water and fish caught fresh from the reservoir, to bubbling cauldrons of thinly-sliced lamb, the hilly suburbs of Beijing are home to some of the city's best-loved weekend escapes - and they're all by the foot of the Great Wall.

programme.title: Foodwise: How to Order Chinese Food
programme.desc: How do you order Chinese food? What's the best way to pair dishes? And what's up with the naming system? In this episode, hosts Tianran and Eva will give you a crash course on everything from deciphering the menu and choosing the right restaurant, to pairing dishes according to color, aroma, taste, and form, so that by the end of the show, you too can feast your friends like a pro.

programme.title: Thrill Seeker’s Guide to China: Mount Siguniang
programme.desc: In this episode, Host Tianran He is on a mission: To reach the summit of Mount Siguniang, a towering snowcapped mountain in southwestern China. Together with his friend Ma Chunlin, a photographer for National Geographic, they trek through one of China’s most beautiful and biodiverse hiking routes, before making for base camp. But with worsening weather conditions and severe altitude sickness, the odds are stacked against them. Ten years ago, Tianran failed his attempt at climbing Mount Siguniang’s First Peak – this time, he’s all in.

programme.title: Pingyao Hand-Polished Lacquer Art
programme.desc: Pingyao is an old town with a history of over 2,700 years in the north of China, in Shanxi Province. The rich cultural heritage it built up over the centuries eventually gave rise to a very special art—Tuiguang lacquerware. This hand-polished lacquerware, unique to Pingyao, is recognized as one of the four representative lacquerwares of China.

programme.title: Beijing Trough Art
programme.desc: When it comes to Beijing’s art, you may picture the Forbidden City and ancient porcelain pieces. In fact, Beijing hosts numerous outstanding modern‑art museums, whose buildings are works of art in their own right. Especially in pivotal period,

programme.title: Ancient tradition in an old town takes on new life
programme.desc: The Chinese lion dance is an art form that combines acrobatics, dance, folk music and embroidery. The southern style lion dance was a staple in the ancient town of Qiantong before the costume faded in the early 20th century. However, now the tradition has been revived by some incredible talented young people from this town.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-4 Medieval Combat
programme.desc: Episode 4 centres on Shanghai, where Eastern and Western martial traditions meet in surprising ways. Kleber visits local practitioners of Historical European Martial Arts (HEMA), trying German and Italian longsword techniques first-hand and seeing how Chinese enthusiasts innovate by combining European swordsmanship with ancient Chinese military manuals. The episode also follows the rise of BUHURT, a European-style armored combat sport now gaining traction in China, featuring locally made traditional Chinese armour in intense full-contact bouts. Through Kleber’s own multicultural martial arts studio and the city’s everyday East-West blend, the episode shows Shanghai as a global hub, a place where classical martial arts find new life and cross-cultural practices thrive.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Taiji Quan
programme.desc: Kleber travels to Chenjiagou in central China, the birthplace of Chen-style Taiji Quan. Also known as tai chi, the martial art is iconic for its slow movements and purported health benefits, but Kleber quickly discovers it’s the real deal: From being thrown around by teenagers to learning about the martial art’s Yin-Yang philosophy, Kleber experiences for himself how this kungfu style allows small to defeat big, and soft to beat strong.

programme.title: Back to the Land: Exploring new life in rural China
programme.desc: When we think of the countryside, what colour comes to mind? The answer lies in Wuxin—Wood, Fire, Earth, Metal and Water—an ancient rhythm that still pulses beneath China’s landscapes. In this programme, we explore how the colours of Wuxing come to life through the traditions of China’s ethnic minority groups. From Lugu Lake’s matrilineal water world and the metal-hued fire of the Yi Torch Festival, to Dali’s earth-driven crafts, Guizhou’s wooden Dong dwellings, and the volcanic winds of Inner Mongolia’s new nomadic life—the Five Elements guide the stories of people reconnecting with land, culture and community.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.2
programme.desc: In this episode, Tianran He continues his journey through Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. He meets the Lhoba people, China’s smallest ethnic group, who show him their traditional hunting attire. At a sacred lake, Tianran visits an ancient monastery and witnesses the 3000-year-old dance of the Gongbo Tibetans, before venturing to a remote village to experience their their way of life.

programme.title: Valentine's with the Snake King
programme.desc: Every year on Qixi Festival (Chinese Valentine's Day), the town of Zhanghu in Nanping, Fujian Province hosts a folk custom event: the Snake King Parade. Local residents carry the statue of the Snake King from the temple, along with specially prepared pythons, and parade through the streets and alleys. Wherever the procession passes, residents and shopkeepers greet them with firecrackers and burn incense to pray, hoping to receive the unique blessing of the Snake King. This distinctive folk activity is rarely seen elsewhere across China. Taking this event as its highlight, the episode leads audiences on a deep dive into the towns and villages of northern Fujian, delivering a highly engaging and visually appealing viewing experience.

programme.title: Young star on green field
programme.desc: Meet Sun Chenxi, a 12-year-old soccer "star" from Ningbo who first picked up the game at the age of four and is already playing with the confidence of a veteran. Sun says he dreams of turning pro one day and leading China's men's team to the World Cup. Dribbling and passing with skills far more advanced than his age, he has won over audiences far and wide after his father posted clips of Sun flashing his fancy footwork on social media. The soon-to-be-teenager now has millions of followers online. Already recognized as a rising young talent, high hopes are pinned on Sun as he is cheered on by his parents, friends, and throngs of fans online.

programme.title: Foodwise: Eat Like Confucius
programme.desc: "Foodwise" host Tianran He explores the ancient town of Qufu, the birthplace of Confucius. Together with a 75th generation descendant of the sage himself, they explore one of Confucius' lifelong pursuits: To cultivate oneself for the betterment of others. From the sophisticated cooking techniques and dining etiquette of Confucian cuisine, which was used for banqueting emperors and aristocrats, to getting hands on with street food and local delicacies, Tianran explores how Confucius' ideals have permeated through modern day life in Qufu.

programme.title: Foodwise: The Herbal Way of Life
programme.desc: 'Medicine tonic is not as good as food tonic' - this Chinese saying is a core principle of Traditional Chinese Medicine. It is believed that the right diet and nourishment can cure and reverse the effects of disease, hence considered an inevitable source of natural health preservation. In this Foodwise episode, our hosts Tianran He and Eva May explore this ancient concept of medicine food homology as they taste their way through different herbs, chat to experts of Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) and visit venues that combine conventional principles with modern ideas. Dive deep into the origin of TCM with us as we learn about the harmony between man and nature and its application in modern functional foods!

programme.title: Thrill Seeker’s Guide to China: Changbai Mountain
programme.desc: An unforgettable adventure awaits amidst the breathtaking landscapes of Changbai Mountain! Embark on a trek through pristine birch forests, marvel at the majestic power of the world's deepest volcanic waterfall, and uncover hidden gems along the way. Indulge in soaking in natural hot springs and experience exhilarating rafting down mountain streams, all leading to the final destination: Tianchi Lake, a heavenly oasis nestled in the clouds. Join the Travelogue team as we delve into the beauty of Changbai Mountain.

programme.title: A Journey on Water from Wuxi to Yixing
programme.desc: Water has always been the heartbeat of human civilization, nurturing the cities it weaves through. In eastern China, the Grand Canal flows across Wuxi, etching its path through history. From the first drops that carved out its waterways to the thriving metropolis we see today, Wuxi’s story is inseparable from the canal that gave it life. In this episode, we uncover how the waters have shaped Wuxi’s identity. This is the story of a city born of water, where the past and present flow together, crafting the future.

programme.title: The Spirit of Zhaxidele
programme.desc: In this episode of Travelogue, Tianran backpacks the way to the western reaches of China. Making his home in beautiful Jiaju village, he learns just what it takes to be a Tibetan, then reaches the mysterious village of Suopo and explore its ancient watchtowers.

programme.title: Young star on green field
programme.desc: Meet Sun Chenxi, a 12-year-old soccer "star" from Ningbo who first picked up the game at the age of four and is already playing with the confidence of a veteran. Sun says he dreams of turning pro one day and leading China's men's team to the World Cup. Dribbling and passing with skills far more advanced than his age, he has won over audiences far and wide after his father posted clips of Sun flashing his fancy footwork on social media. The soon-to-be-teenager now has millions of followers online. Already recognized as a rising young talent, high hopes are pinned on Sun as he is cheered on by his parents, friends, and throngs of fans online.

programme.title: Kung Fu Wise II-5 Xingyi Quan
programme.desc: In Episode 5, Kleber travels to Shanxi, the birthplace of Xingyi Quan. He follows Master Cao Bing, a traditional Xingyi Quan teacher and modern combat coach, from Taiyuan’s noodle shops to Pingyao’s ancient streets, and from centuries-old vinegar breweries to Guandi temples, exploring Xingyi Quan’s deep links to Shanxi’s merchant culture and history. Master Cao breaks down the core principles of the “Five Elements and Twelve Forms”, and shows in his modern gym how techniques found in Muay Thai and Jiu-Jitsu can be integrated without losing Xingyi Quan’s philosophical core, guided by the mindset of taking only what is essential. Set against ancient sites such as Yongle Palace, this episode shows how true inner strength is like traditional timber architecture: stable, balanced, and built from within.

programme.title: Kungfu-wise: Taiji Quan
programme.desc: Kleber travels to Chenjiagou in central China, the birthplace of Chen-style Taiji Quan. Also known as tai chi, the martial art is iconic for its slow movements and purported health benefits, but Kleber quickly discovers it’s the real deal: From being thrown around by teenagers to learning about the martial art’s Yin-Yang philosophy, Kleber experiences for himself how this kungfu style allows small to defeat big, and soft to beat strong.

programme.desc: Frontline Chinese scientists on the Qinghai–Tibet Plateau enter the spotlight, showcasing a spirit of exploration and dedication alongside the vital contributions their expeditions make to China's ecological progress and global conservation.

programme.title: The Adventure in Powder Snow Ep.1
programme.desc: Centered on ice and snow sports and ice-snow cultural tourism, this documentary visits high-quality ski resorts and distinctive ice-snow destinations across China, presenting the development of the ice-snow industry and public ice-snow lifestyles. It records the experiences of ice-snow enthusiasts and the perseverance of practitioners, conveying the charm and vitality of ice-snow sports.

programme.title: The Adventure in Powder Snow Ep.1
programme.desc: Centered on ice and snow sports and ice-snow cultural tourism, this documentary visits high-quality ski resorts and distinctive ice-snow destinations across China, presenting the development of the ice-snow industry and public ice-snow lifestyles. It records the experiences of ice-snow enthusiasts and the perseverance of practitioners, conveying the charm and vitality of ice-snow sports.

programme.title: Lancang-Mekong: The River That Flows South Ep.1
programme.desc: This documentary follows 24 families along the Lancang-Mekong River—their lives, their connections, and the quiet ways they remain independent yet bound to one another. What emerges is a portrait of shared wisdom, shaped by the river and passed down for generations.

programme.title: Blue Helmets, No Borders Ep.1
programme.desc: As the first ever comprehensive documentary on Chinese UN peacekeepers, this documentary charts their missions in South Sudan and Lebanon. Despite the harsh conditions, the cameras capture the reality of the work these brave people do - as builders and humanitarians as well as peace-keeping soldiers in conflict zones.

programme.title: My Village is a National Treasure Ep.1
programme.desc: Dingcun village in Shanxi has a deep cultural legacy borne from a long historical tradition. Yet despite its rich resources, the village remained stagnant for years, described by local scholars as “begging for food while holding a golden bowl.” Now, Dingcun is charting a new course, developing its rural tourism by embracing its unique cultural assets.

programme.title: My Village is a National Treasure Ep.2
programme.desc: Dingcun village in Shanxi has a deep cultural legacy borne from a long historical tradition. programme.title: Journey of a Legendary Landscape Painting
programme.desc: This documentary provides an immersive look into the intangible cultural heritage techniques woven into Chinese dance drama. Through in-depth interviews with the directors, lead performers, and other key creatives, this film takes overseas audiences deep into the elegant and refined aesthetic spirit of contemporary Chinese performing arts.

programme.title: City in Blossom — Hangzhou's Osmanthus Season
programme.desc: Through 4K and macro photography, this documentary unfolds the thousand-year romance between Hangzhou and the osmanthus flower, a love story steeped in fragrance, rooted in history and radiant with striking color.

programme.title: Biodiversity on Earth
programme.desc: This story follows the migration of Tibetan antelopes as they make their way through the majestic Hoh Xil heartland for calving season, offering a glimpse into the hidden animal kingdom around Zonag Lake.

programme.title: You're not Alone Ep.1
programme.desc: As the number of people with autism continues to grow, China's educational resources, social support systems, public awareness and acceptance remain far from adequate. Following two children with autism, this documentary reveals the realities faced by the community and their families, as well as the unremitting efforts and difficult choices they must make in the face of adversity.

programme.title: You're not Alone Ep.1
programme.desc: As the number of people with autism continues to grow, China's educational resources, social support systems, public awareness and acceptance remain far from adequate. Following two children with autism, this documentary reveals the realities faced by the community and their families, as well as the unremitting efforts and difficult choices they must make in the face of adversity.

programme.title: You're not Alone Ep.2
programme.desc: As the number of people with autism continues to grow, China's educational resources, social support systems, public awareness and acceptance remain far from adequate. programme.title: China, Race to the Future Ep.1
programme.desc: This five-episode series explores China's growing cultural confidence, showing how a revival of traditional culture is fueling "chain reactions" that are reshaping Chinese manufacturing and development. In particular, the series stresses how China’s immense cultural capital is empowering a uniquely Chinese approach to building a future that puts openness, globalization and the environment center-stage!

programme.title: Sunrise over the Plateau (Special Edition)

programme.desc: Pastor Larry Huch instructs viewers on how to enter into a realm of blessing and revelation through the studies of the Jewish roots of the Christian faith.

programme.title: It is Written
programme.desc: The It Is Written team is dedicated to spreading the Gospel and promoting spiritual growth across the world by preaching from the Bible, spreading a message of hope, and encouraging a relationship with Jesus.

programme.title: The Jewish Jesus
programme.desc: Rabbi Schneider reveals the deeper meaning behind God's commandments and their relevance in the modern world and discusses the Torah's wisdom, divine blessing, and practical guidance for living in truth and healing.

programme.title: The Winner's Way with Mac Hammond
programme.desc: As our faith, courage, and discipline strengthen, we become vessels through which God can bring blessings to others. Discover more about this transformative journey on The Winner's Way with Mac Hammond.

programme.title: Ron Carpenter
programme.desc: Ron Carpenter is the Pastor for Redemption Bay Area in San Jose California. He is also the host of Ron Carpenter Television which airs weekly both nationally and internationally worldwide via television, internet and various satellites.

programme.title: James A. McMenis
programme.desc: Pastor James McMenis shares Jesus Christ's love with those lost, to bring clarity to the Word of God and make it relevant to people everywhere.

programme.title: Israel: The Prophetic Connection
programme.desc: Reverend Doctor John Tweedie provides a detailed analysis of biblical prophecies concerning the past, present and future Israel through vivid imagery, on-site teaching and interviews that help viewers gain insight into God's plans for the world.

programme.title: Your Miraculous Life With Dr. Kynan Bridges
programme.desc: Dr. Kynan Bridges reveals practical, biblical keys to hearing God's voice, growing in spiritual maturity, and walking in lasting victory.

programme.title: Sid Roth - ¡Es Sobrenatural!
programme.desc: Hal Sacks era un judío ortodoxo que filmó el Movimiento de Jesús, presenciando vidas transformadas por milagros, pero se negaba a creer. Entonces, un momento sobrenatural rompió su resistencia y le abrió los ojos a la verdad del Mesías.

programme.title: Joni Table Talk
programme.desc: Joni, un programa de entrevistas de media hora animado y, a veces, impredecible, aborda una amplia gama de temas relevantes, temas controvertidos y temas de actualidad con franqueza e ingenio. Combinando segmentos vibrantes con invitados notables.

programme.title: Ministry Now
programme.desc: Al frente de la nueva normalidad de hoy, la familia Lamb lleva a los espectadores de todo el mundo a Ministry Now. programme.title: Pastor Sam Rodriguez

programme.title: Dios de Milagros
programme.desc: Experimente el poder de Dios en su vida y prepárese para recibir sanidad, restauración, y milagros mientras recibe una palabra llena de unción y poder predicada por el pastor Hernan Castaño desde la Iglesia Ríos De Aceite en Houston, Texas a las naciones. Su vida no será la misma! En Dios De Milagros, los enfermos son sanados, los cautivos liberados, los oprimidos ministrados, y todos crecen espiritualmente con las enseñanzas y predicaciones de los pastores Hernan y Wendy Castaño. Es tiempo de ver el mover de Dios en tu vida y en tu familia!

programme.desc: Un libro antiguo transporta a los dos niños que lo encontraron y a su robot al pasado, donde experimentan una serie de aventuras emocionantes y extraordinarias en medio de hechos bíblicos.

programme.title: Torchlighters
programme.desc: Cada episodio de Torchlighters presenta la historia de un héroe de la vida real de la historia cristiana. Los niños verán lo que Dios puede hacer a través de un "encendedor de antorchas" y serán desafiados a llevar su propia antorcha mientras aprenden sob

programme.title: Adoración en casa
programme.desc: Transforme la atmósfera de lo que sea que esté enfrentando a través del poder de la adoración con Josh Morales.

programme.title: Fe y Fuego
programme.desc: Afán versus fe. A veces, pensamos que si algo es arriesgado no es de Dios, si la duda nos visita buscamos mil confirmaciones para decidirnos a comenzar algo, pero vale la pena depositar toda nuestra confianza en la fe que Dios nos ha dado.

programme.title: En Familia con Luis y Kristen

programme.title: A Toda Luz
programme.desc: ¿Te hace falta ánimo o quieres crecer en tu fe junto a otros? A Toda Luz es un programa lleno de vida, con conversaciones reales, risas, y verdades que inspiran. Con voces dinámicas y temas relevantes, te anima, te conecta y te acerca más a Dios.

Su vida no será la misma! En Dios De Milagros, los enfermos son sanados, los cautivos liberados, los oprimidos ministrados, y todos crecen espiritualmente con las enseñanzas y predicaciones de los pastores Hernan y Wendy Castaño. Es tiempo de ver el mover de Dios en tu vida y en tu familia!
